method_atom: A limit-theorem technique that shows the empirical distribution of a large ensemble's spectrum converges almost surely to a deterministic limiting measure: one uses logarithmic potential theory in the complex plane to reconstruct the limiting measure from the log-determinant / Stieltjes-type transform of the spectrum, and controls the extreme (especially smallest) singular values of shifted operators under only a finite fourth-moment condition to justify the convergence.
object_atom: The eigenvalues of large complex random matrices with independent, identically distributed entries of mean zero and variance 1/n, whose empirical spectral distribution converges to the uniform law on the unit disk (the circular law).
brief_reason: The potential-theory-plus-extreme-singular-value machinery for pinning down an almost-sure limiting spectral measure under a moment condition is transferable, while the i.i.d. random matrix ensemble and the resulting circular (uniform-on-the-disk) law are the linear-algebra/probability-specific object.
